factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable with no significant changes in debt facilities.

evidence.text: We have historically generated substantial cash from operations, which has enabled us to fund our organic growth while paying cash dividends and repurchasing stock. In addition, we maintain the following debt facilities as described in Note 4, Financing Arrangements...

factors.summary: Free cash flow is declining due to increased net operating working capital.

evidence.text: Cash flow from operating activities. Our operating cash flows benefited in the prior year from declining freight rates in ocean and truckload services, which resulted in a decrease in net operating working capital and drove strong operating cash flow. In the current year, freight rates in ocean services have increased resulting in an increase in net operating working capital driving a decline in operating cash flows in the three months ended March 31, 2024, compared to the three months ended March 31, 2023.

factors.summary: CapEx is focused on software investments to improve customer and carrier experience.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures consisted primarily of investments in software, which are intended to develop and deliver scalable solutions by transforming our processes, accelerate the pace of development and prioritizing data integrity, improve our customer and carrier experience, and increase efficiency to help expand our adjusted operating margins and grow the business.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are stable with no share repurchases in the first quarter of 2024.

evidence.text: No shares were repurchased during the first quarter of 2024. The number of shares we repurchase, if any, during future periods will vary based on our cash position, other potential uses of our cash, and market conditions.

factors.summary: M&A activity is focused on restructuring with no specific mention of new acquisitions.

evidence.text: In addition to the above, Global Forwarding personnel expenses for 2024 included $3.2 million of severance and related personnel expenses associated with our 2024 Restructuring Program.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

factors.summary: Economic conditions are stable with no specific mention of expansion or slowdown.

evidence.text: The global forwarding market has shown improvement from the weak consumer demand experienced in the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

factors.summary: FX exposure is stable with no specific mention of favorable or unfavorable movements.

evidence.text: As of March 31, 2024, we were in compliance with all of the covenants under our debt agreements.

factors.summary: The global forwarding market is improving due to increased ocean volumes and elevated e-commerce demand.

evidence.text: Our first quarter of 2024 global forwarding results were significantly impacted by the global disruptions discussed in the market trends section. Specifically, we experienced a 23.0 percent increase in air freight tonnage, driven by ocean freight conversions in many trade lanes.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through better execution and pricing discipline, leading to sequential monthly improvements.

evidence.text: ...driven by our improved execution and pricing discipline within our portfolio. Our average truckload linehaul cost per mile and linehaul rate charged to our customers, excluding fuel surcharges, both decreased approximately 7.5 percent during the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is low, with excess carrier capacity leading to soft market conditions.

evidence.text: Despite modest decreases in carrier counts, there remains an excess of carrier capacity, leading to continued soft market conditions.

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to challenges in market conditions and disruptions.

evidence.text: Despite the challenging market conditions, our combined NAST truckload and LTL volumes, along with our adjusted gross profits per transaction, showed sequential improvement each month within the first quarter of 2024, driven by improved execution and pricing discipline within our portfolio.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include global disruptions, geopolitical conflicts, and economic uncertainties affecting freight pricing and availability.

evidence.text: The challenges facing the ocean freight market are leading to increased ocean freight conversions, which alongside elevated e-commerce demand out of North Asia, have led to sharp increases in the cost of air freight in certain trade lanes.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is focused on integrating supply chain solutions and improving operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Sourcing adjusted gross profits increased, driven by integrated supply chain solutions for retail customers.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include global disruptions, geopolitical conflicts, and economic uncertainties affecting freight pricing and availability.

evidence.text: The challenges facing the ocean freight market are leading to increased ocean freight conversions, which alongside elevated e-commerce demand out of North Asia, have led to sharp increases in the cost of air freight in certain trade lanes.

factors.summary: Catalysts include sequential monthly improvements in adjusted gross profits per transaction driven by better execution and pricing discipline.

evidence.text: Our combined NAST truckload and LTL volumes along with our adjusted gross profits per transaction showed sequential improvement each month within the first quarter of 2024, driven by improved execution and pricing discipline within our portfolio.

factors.summary: R&D investment is focused on internally developed software, with some impairment losses noted.

evidence.text: Direct internally developed software amortization 10,222 7,317. We also incurred a $5.0 million impairment loss in other SG&A expenses primarily related to capitalized internally developed software.

factors.summary: Digital automation is a key focus area, with technology built by and for supply chain experts.

evidence.text: We bring together customers, carriers, and suppliers to connect and grow supply chains. We are grounded in our customer promise to use our technology, which is built by and for supply chain experts and powered by our information advantage, to deliver smarter solutions.

factors.summary: Headcount is being reduced as part of cost optimization efforts, with a 11.3% decrease in average employee headcount.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased 1.0 percent to $379.1 million, primarily due to cost optimization efforts and lower average employee headcount, which decreased 11.3 percent, partially offset by higher restructuring charges related to workforce reductions.

evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure and compressing, driven by lower pricing in truckload services and decreased adjusted gross profits, leading to a significant decline in operating margins.

evidence.text: NAST adjusted gross profits decreased due to lower pricing in truckload services, resulting in lower adjusted gross profits per transaction.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: Adjusted operating margin of 19.3 percent declined 420 basis points. Income from operations decreased 21.1 percent to $127.1 million, due to decreased adjusted gross profits.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: Pricing power is weak due to soft market conditions, which have led to lower pricing in truckload services and limited spot market opportunities at attractive rates. Freight rates are suppressed, with most shipments moving under committed pricing agreements, further indicating a lack of pricing strength.

evidence.text: The lower adjusted gross profit per transaction was driven by the soft market conditions discussed in the market trends and business trends sections above, resulting in lower pricing and limited spot market opportunities at attractive rates in the first quarter of 2024.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: Most shipments moved under committed pricing agreements and suppressed freight rates for the limited number of shipments reaching the spot market.

factors.summary: Demand trends are negative, with both stable and decelerating demand observed in different subsections. The market is characterized by weak consumer demand and excess carrier capacity, which have led to suppressed freight rates.

evidence.text: The soft market conditions, characterized by weak demand and excess carrier capacity, continue to suppress freight rates in the surface transportation market as discussed in the market and business trends section above.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: The first quarter of 2024 surface transportation results were significantly impacted by the prevailing soft market conditions... Industry freight volumes decreased in the first quarter of 2024 compared to the same period in 2023.

factors.summary: Volumes are showing a positive trend with a 1.5% increase in combined truckload and LTL volumes. Despite some challenges, there are sequential monthly improvements in unit volumes.

evidence.text: Partially offsetting the decline was an increase of 1.5 percent in combined NAST truckload and LTL volumes.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: Our combined North American Surface Transportation ('NAST') truckload and less than truckload ('LTL') volumes increased by 1.5 percent during the first quarter of 2024 compared to the first quarter of 2023. In addition, our combined NAST truckload and LTL volumes along with our adjusted gross profits per transaction showed sequential improvement each month within the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Cost optimization efforts are partially offsetting cost increases, with reductions in personnel expenses driven by lower headcount and efficiency initiatives. However, these savings are being partially offset by an increase in stock-based compensation expense.

evidence.text: Cost optimization efforts, including lower average headcount in addition to lower commission expenses, were mostly offset by an increase in stock-based compensation expense.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased primarily driven by cost optimization efforts, including lower average employee headcount. These reductions were partially offset by an increase in stock-based compensation expense...

factors.summary: Organic growth is mixed with sequential improvement in volumes but accompanied by decelerating revenue growth due to lower pricing and challenging market conditions.

evidence.text: Despite the challenging market conditions our combined NAST truckload and LTL volumes, along with our adjusted gross profits per transaction, showed sequential improvement each month within the first quarter of 2024, driven by improved execution and pricing discipline within our portfolio.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: Total revenues decreased 4.3 percent to $4.4 billion, primarily driven by lower pricing in our truckload services, partially offset by higher pricing and increased volume in our ocean services.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting in multiple directions, with growth in higher-margin ocean services and increased LTL volumes, but also facing declines in intermodal and warehousing adjusted gross profits, as well as reduced pricing and lower purchased transportation costs in truckload services.

evidence.text: NAST other adjusted gross profits decreased, primarily driven by a decline in intermodal adjusted gross profits per transaction and decreased intermodal and warehousing adjusted gross profits. These decreases were partially offset by an increase in LTL volumes.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues and direct costs decreased primarily due to reduced pricing and lower purchased transportation costs in truckload services. This decline was partially offset by increased volume and higher pricing and purchased transportation costs in ocean services.

factors.summary: Input costs are showing a consistent decline across different areas, with both fuel surcharges and transportation costs decreasing. This trend is evident in lower linehaul costs and rates, as well as reduced purchased transportation expenses.

evidence.text: Our average truckload per mile linehaul cost and linehaul rate charged to customers, which excludes fuel surcharges, both decreased approximately 7.5 percent in the first quarter of 2024 compared to the first quarter of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: Purchased transportation and related services decreased primarily due to reduced pricing and lower purchased transportation costs in truckload services.

factors.summary: Labor costs have shown mixed trends, with stable personnel expenses in the first quarter comparison but a decrease in the broader operating performance due to cost optimization and workforce reductions.

evidence.text: NAST personnel expenses were essentially flat compared to the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased 1.0 percent to $379.1 million, primarily due to cost optimization efforts and lower average employee headcount, which decreased 11.3 percent, partially offset by higher restructuring charges related to workforce reductions.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: The company's market share is stable, with no indication of gains or losses. There is no explicit mention of market share in the Management's Discussion and Analysis section.

evidence.text: We have historically generated substantial cash from operations, which has enabled us to fund our organic growth while paying cash dividends and repurchasing stock.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is showing mixed signals, with internal improvements in execution and cost optimization, but facing external challenges from global disruptions affecting waterways and schedule reliability.

evidence.text: Despite the challenging market conditions our combined NAST truckload and LTL volumes, along with our adjusted gross profits per transaction, showed sequential improvement each month within the first quarter of 2024, driven by improved execution and pricing discipline within our portfolio.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: The global forwarding market faced significant disruptions limiting the availability of several of the world’s most significant waterways. These disruptions have resulted in increased re-routing and degrading schedule reliability for ocean shipments.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are mixed, with some evidence of improvement in certain markets but ongoing challenges in others. While the global forwarding market has shown improvement, the North America surface transportation market continues to face soft conditions due to excess capacity and pricing pressure.

evidence.text: The global forwarding market has shown improvement from the weak consumer demand experienced in the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2024, Compared to the Three Months Ended March 31, 2023

evidence.text: The North America surface transportation market continues to gradually move toward a balance of carrier supply and shipper demand. Despite modest decreases in carrier counts, there remains an excess of carrier capacity, leading to continued soft market conditions.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is a mixed topic with some sections indicating stability but others not providing explicit information. The liquidity and capital resources section mentions stable concentration, while the introduction/MDA section does not address it.

evidence.text: We have historically generated substantial cash from operations, which has enabled us to fund our organic growth while paying cash dividends and repurchasing stock.

factors.summary: The company maintains differentiation through technology, customer relationships, and integrated supply chain solutions. Investments in software and technology aim to enhance scalability, efficiency, and customer experience, while also expanding margins and business growth.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures consisted primarily of investments in software, which are intended to develop and deliver scalable solutions by transforming our processes, accelerate the pace of development and prioritizing data integrity, improve our customer and carrier experience, and increase efficiency to help expand our adjusted operating margins and grow the business.
We bring together customers, carriers, and suppliers to connect and grow supply chains. We are grounded in our customer promise to use our technology... to deliver smarter solutions.
